ansible.scm.git_publish.short_description: Publish changes from a repository available on the execution node to a distant location
ansible.scm.git_publish.description: Publish changes from a repository available on the execution node to a distant location

include.description: A list of files to include (add) in the commit

open_browser.description: Open the default browser to the pull-request page

remove.description: Remove the local copy of the repository if the push is successful

timeout.description: The timeout in seconds for each command issued

token.description:
The token to use to authenticate to the origin repository If provided, an 'http.extraheader' will be added to the commands interacting with the origin repository
 Will only be used for https based connections

tag.description: Specify the tag details associated with the commit.

user.description: Details for the user to be used for the commit Will only be used if not already configured

ssh_key_file.description: Path to the SSH private key file to use for authentication with git. Used only for SSH-based repository URLs (e.g., git@github.com:...).

ssh_key_content.description: The content of the SSH private key for authentication with git. Ideal for use with Ansible Vault or other secret management systems. Used only for SSH-based repository URLs.

ansible.scm.git_retrieve.short_description: Retrieve a repository from a distant location and make it available on the execution node
ansible.scm.git_retrieve.description: Retrieve a repository from a distant location and make it available on the execution node

branch.description: Details about the new branch that will be created

host_key_checking.description: Configure strict host key checking for ssh based connections accept-new will accept new host keys (StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new) no will disable strict host key checking (StrictHostKeyChecking=no) system will use the global system setting and not configure the git repository yes will enable strict host key checking (StrictHostKeyChecking=yes)

parent_directory.description: The local directory where the repository will be placed If the parent directory does not exist, it will be created

timeout.description: The timeout in seconds for each command issued
